damavand.subtitle: Iran & the Middle East's highest peak — Mazandaran / Tehran
damavand.about: Mount Damavand at 5,610 m is Iran's highest peak, the highest in the Middle East, and one of the world's highest volcanoes. This dormant stratovolcano in Mazandaran province is a national symbol of Iran. Its sulfur crater, permanent snowfields and extraordinary summit views make every ascent unforgettable.
damavand.description: Iran's highest peak and a dormant volcano with a legendary sulfur crater and year-round snow.

alamkooh.subtitle: Iran's 2nd highest peak — Mazandaran
alamkooh.about: Alam Kuh (4,850 m) is Iran's second highest mountain, famous for its spectacular northern granite wall — a classic destination for technical alpine climbing in the Central Alborz.
alamkooh.description: Iran's second highest peak with the Middle East's most famous alpine wall.

tochal.subtitle: Tehran's nearest major summit
tochal.about: Tochal (3,933 m) is the most accessible major peak near Tehran, popular with city climbers via hiking trails or the Tochal telecabin.
tochal.description: Tehran's favourite day-hike peak with telecabin access and city views.

takht-soleiman.description: Iran's third highest peak (4,643 m) on the Mazandaran–Tehran border with multiple ascent routes.

siah-sang.description: A 4,604 m summit in Central Alborz named for its dark rock faces and snowy ridges.

azadkooh.description: A prominent 4,435 m peak in Central Alborz, popular for multi-day traverses.

sabalan.description: Northwest Iran's iconic volcano (4,811 m) with a crater lake at the summit.

dena.description: The 'Lady of Iranian mountains' — a long ridge with multiple summits above 4,000 m.

zardkooh.description: Highest peak of the Zagros range (4,548 m) in the heart of Bakhtiari country.

hezar.description: Southern Iran's highest peak (4,465 m) with views over the Lut desert.

eshterankoh.description: Lorestan's 'camel-back' mountain with green meadows and waterfalls.

karkas.description: Popular Isfahan peak with desert views on clear days.

sahand.description: Dormant volcano in East Azerbaijan with wildflower meadows in spring.

taftan.description: Iran's only semi-active volcano with sulfur steam at the crater.

shirkoh.description: Yazd's highest peak rising dramatically from the desert.
